reasoning: Peer set (Denzi helmets and shields) exhibits sophisticated multi-tone gradient shading with clear 3D volumetric form — every peer uses 4-6 distinct shading steps to convey curvature and material. Outline weight is consistent (1px dark), and outline color matches. Shadow direction appears roughly correct (top-left light source), but shadow count is lower — peers show distinct ambient-occlusion-style darkening at recessed areas that boots lack. Highlight intensity on peers is strong and specular (especially the gold and teal helmets); boots show only a modest warm-orange highlight strip with no secondary bounce light. Detail density on peers includes rivets, engravings, face-plate geometry, and shield boss detail; boots show almost no surface detail beyond color zoning. Proportion is plausible for the item type. Palette overlap is not penalised (different item type = different hue family is acceptable). Charm level is the clearest failure: peers project 'inventory-quality RPG equipment' refinement; boots read as a rough draft or placeholder at the current rendering resolution. The cumulative gap in rendering sophistication and detail density pushes this below the marginal threshold.
